title: epipactis albensis var. fibri
intro: Epipactis albensis var. fibri, also known as the European helleborine, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to Europe. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and other secondary metabolites. The health benefits of Epipactis albensis var. fibri include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and exhibit antioxidant activity, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying the leaves and roots, then infusing them in hot water or using them in tincture form, although the use of this plant for medicinal purposes should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
